---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \usepackage{tikz}
  \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta, shapes.geometric, positioning, decorations.pathreplacing}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commented) -->

```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{5.1 - Test Interpretation: Introduction}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```

## Applied Psychometrics: the key word

The course is on \textbf{Applied} Psychometrics

\vfill \pause

Application always implies a \textbf{purpose} \pause and in clinical and research contexts, that purpose is the \textbf{interpretation} of test results.

\vfill \pause

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}
  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=gray!15, minimum width=3.5cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center] (test) {\textbf{Test}};
  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=gray!15, minimum width=3.5cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center, right=2.5cm of test] (interp) {\textbf{Interpretation}};
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick] (test) -- (interp);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```


## Scores are Interpreted, Not Read

A score is not a label to be looked up in a table.

\vfill \pause

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}

  % "read/convert" node — crossed out
  \node[inner sep=6pt, outer sep=0pt] (read) at (0,0) {%
    \parbox{4cm}{\centering \emph{read} / \emph{convert}}%
  };
  \onslide<2->{
  \draw[red, thick] ([yshift=1em, xshift=2em]read.south west) -- ([yshift=1em, xshift=-2em]read.south east);
 }
  \onslide<3->{

  % "interpret" node — highlighted
  \node[inner sep=6pt, outer sep=0pt, draw=mDarkTeal, rounded corners, fill=mDarkTeal!10] (interp) at (6,0) {%
    \parbox{4cm}{\centering \textbf{interpret}}%
  };
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick, mDarkTeal] (read) -- (interp);
}
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

\vfill \pause

\small \emph{Interpretation} stresses the \textbf{active role} of the psychologist. It requires \emph{judgment}, not mere table lookup.


## No Straightforward Conversion

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}

  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=gray!15, minimum width=2.5cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center] (score) {\textbf{Score}};

  % Single arrow fantasy
  \node[draw, dashed, rounded corners, fill=red!8, minimum width=2.5cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center, right=3cm of score] (wrong) {\small \emph{single} \\ \emph{interpretation}};
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, dashed, red!70!black] (score) -- node[above]{\small\color{red!70!black}not this} (wrong);
  \draw[red, thick] ([yshift=0.35em]wrong.south west) -- ([yshift=0.35em]wrong.south east);

  \pause

  % Multiple arrows
  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=mDarkTeal!10, minimum width=2.5cm, minimum height=0.8cm, align=center, right=3cm of score, yshift=2.5cm] (i1) {\small Interpretation A};
  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=mDarkTeal!10, minimum width=2.5cm, minimum height=0.8cm, align=center, right=3cm of score, yshift=1.25cm] (i2) {\small Interpretation B};
  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=mDarkTeal!10, minimum width=2.5cm, minimum height=0.8cm, align=center, right=3cm of score, yshift=-2cm] (i3) {\small Interpretation C};

  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick, mDarkTeal] (score) -- (i1.west);
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick, mDarkTeal] (score) -- (i2.west);
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick, mDarkTeal] (score) -- (i3.west);

\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

\vfill

The \emph{same score} can carry different meanings depending on context.


## What Determines Test Interpretation?

Two main dimensions shape how a score is interpreted:

\vfill \pause

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}

  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=gray!15, minimum width=2.8cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center] (score) {Score};

  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=red!8, minimum width=4.2cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center, above right=0cm and 1cm of score] (clin)
    {\parbox{4cm}{\centering \small \textbf{Integration with \\ other clinical information}}};

  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=red!8, minimum width=4.2cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center, below right=0cm and 1cm of score] (test)
    {\parbox{4cm}{\centering \small \textbf{Strengths and limitations \\ of the test}}};

\onslide<3->{
  \node[draw, rounded corners, fill=orange!20, minimum width=2.8cm, minimum height=1cm, align=center, right=6cm of score] (interp)
    {Interpretation};
}

  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick] (score) -- (clin);
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick] (score) -- (test);
  
  \onslide<3>{
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick] (clin) -- (interp);
  \draw[-{Stealth[length=3mm]}, thick] (test) -- (interp);
}
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```


## Integration with Clinical Information

![](Figures/Integration.png){fig-align="center" width="60%"}

## Psychometric proprerties of the tests

![](Figures/Psychometric_properties.png){fig-align="center" width="60%"}
